Phase 2 of split-jjd-subfiles: Transform the main file and update documentation.

**Context:** Phase 1 just completed. 19 subfiles now exist in Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/:
- Routines: JJSRLD-load.adoc, JJSRSV-save.adoc, JJSRPS-persist.adoc, JJSRWP-wrap.adoc
- Commands: JJSCNO-nominate.adoc, JJSCSL-slate.adoc, JJSCRL-rail.adoc, JJSCDR-draft.adoc, JJSCTL-tally.adoc, JJSCFU-furlough.adoc, JJSCRT-retire.adoc, JJSCVL-validate.adoc, JJSCMU-muster.adoc, JJSCSD-saddle.adoc, JJSCSC-scout.adoc, JJSCPD-parade.adoc, JJSCRN-rein.adoc, JJSCNC-notch.adoc, JJSCCH-chalk.adoc

**Step 1: Rename JJD to JJSA**
```bash
mv Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJD-GallopsData.adoc Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSA-GallopsData.adoc
```

**Step 2: Replace procedure bodies with include directives**

In JJSA-GallopsData.adoc, for each routine and command section:
- Keep the `[[anchor]]` line
- Keep the `// ⟦annotation⟧` line  
- Keep the `=== {header}` or `==== {header}` line
- Replace the procedure body (everything after header until next anchor/section) with an include directive

Pattern for each section:
```
[[jjdr_load]]
// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine⟧
=== {jjdr_load}

\include::JJSRLD-load.adoc[]
```

The sections and their include files:
- `[[jjdr_load]]` → `include::JJSRLD-load.adoc[]`
- `[[jjdr_save]]` → `include::JJSRSV-save.adoc[]`
- `[[jjdr_persist]]` → `include::JJSRPS-persist.adoc[]`
- `[[jjdr_wrap]]` → `include::JJSRWP-wrap.adoc[]`
- `[[jjdo_nominate]]` → `include::JJSCNO-nominate.adoc[]`
- `[[jjdo_slate]]` → `include::JJSCSL-slate.adoc[]`
- `[[jjdo_rail]]` → `include::JJSCRL-rail.adoc[]`
- `[[jjdo_draft]]` → `include::JJSCDR-draft.adoc[]`
- `[[jjdo_tally]]` → `include::JJSCTL-tally.adoc[]`
- `[[jjdo_furlough]]` → `include::JJSCFU-furlough.adoc[]`
- `[[jjdo_retire]]` → `include::JJSCRT-retire.adoc[]`
- `[[jjdo_validate]]` → `include::JJSCVL-validate.adoc[]`
- `[[jjdo_muster]]` → `include::JJSCMU-muster.adoc[]`
- `[[jjdo_saddle]]` → `include::JJSCSD-saddle.adoc[]`
- `[[jjdo_scout]]` → `include::JJSCSC-scout.adoc[]`
- `[[jjdo_parade]]` → `include::JJSCPD-parade.adoc[]`
- `[[jjdo_rein]]` → `include::JJSCRN-rein.adoc[]`
- `[[jjdo_notch]]` → `include::JJSCNC-notch.adoc[]`
- `[[jjdo_chalk]]` → `include::JJSCCH-chalk.adoc[]`

**Step 3: Update CLAUDE.md**

In the project root CLAUDE.md, find the JJK Subdirectory section and:

1. Change the JJD line from:
   `- **JJD**  → \`jjk/vov_veiled/JJD-GallopsData.adoc\` (Gallops data model specification)`
   to:
   `- **JJSA** → \`jjk/vov_veiled/JJSA-GallopsData.adoc\` (Gallops data model specification - main file)`

2. Add all 19 new acronym mappings after JJSA (alphabetically by acronym):
   - **JJSCCH** → `jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCCH-chalk.adoc`

   135→
   136→== Overview
   137→
   138→The
   139→{jjdgr_gallops}
   140→is the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
   141→It tracks
   142→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   143→(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
   144→{jjdpr_pace_s}
   145→(discrete actions).
   146→Each
   147→{jjdpr_pace}
   148→maintains a history of
   149→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   150→(plan refinements).
   151→
   152→== Design Principles
   153→
   154→=== Exclusive JSON Ownership
   155→
   156→All access to the
   157→{jjdgr_gallops}
   158→goes through {jjdx_cli}, the Job Jockey subcommand of the Voce Viva executor.
   159→No jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
   160→This is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.
   161→
   162→The rationale: writing reliable bash with jq pipelines requires fighting LLM training data.
   163→The internet is full of bad bash.
   164→Rust replaces fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
   165→The compiler is tireless; humans are not.
   166→
   167→=== Git as Journal
   168→
   169→The
   170→{jjdgr_gallops}
   171→remains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
   172→Every mutation creates a diffable commit.
   173→Git owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.
   174→
   175→The
   176→{jjdkr_tack}
   177→history within each
   178→{jjdpr_pace}
   179→exists for convenient postmortem analysis.
   180→Git has the full history, but
   181→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   182→let us see plan evolution without spelunking commits.
   183→
   184→=== Diff-Friendly Mutations
   185→
   186→New
   187→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   188→prepend to position 0 rather than append.
   189→This produces cleaner git diffs: prepending adds a line with trailing comma, while appending would modify the previous last line to add a comma.
   190→Small discipline, large cumulative benefit.
   191→
   192→The
   193→{jjdhm_order}
   194→array contains full
   195→{jjdt_coronet_s}.
   196→Each
   197→{jjdt_coronet}
   198→embeds its parent
   199→{jjdt_firemark}
   200→(redundant within a single
   201→{jjdhr_heat}).
   202→This redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.
   203→
   204→=== Crash-Safe Architecture
   205→
   206→Bash handles locking via `git update-ref`.
   207→{jjdx_cli} owns all
   208→{jjdgr_gallops}
   209→file access - reading, transforming, and writing.
   210→{jjdx_cli} implements atomic writes internally (write to temp file, then rename).
   211→If {jjdx_cli} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.
   212→
   213→The pattern:
   214→[source,bash]
   215→----
   216→lock_acquire
   217→vvx jjx gallops nominate --file gallops.json "my-heat" "260111" || { lock_release; die; }
   218→lock_release
   219→----
   220→
   221→This keeps bash simple (just locking and invocation) while {jjdx_cli} owns all JSON file operations.
   222→
   223→=== Deterministic Serialization
   224→
   225→Implementations MUST serialize JSON object keys in deterministic order to minimize diff churn.
   226→For `heats` and `paces` objects, use sorted key order (e.g., Rust `BTreeMap` instead of `HashMap`).
   227→This ensures that reordering paces via
   228→{jjdo_rail}
   229→produces minimal diffs — only the `order` array changes, not the entire `paces` object.
   230→
   231→=== Identity Encoding
   232→
   233→{jjdt_firemark}
   234→(`₣` prefix) and
   235→{jjdt_coronet}
   236→(`₢` prefix) are the canonical identities used in JSON keys, CLI arguments, and display.
   237→The base64 encoding provides compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
   238→Internally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.
   239→
   240→=== Horse Racing Vocabulary
   241→
   242→Job Jockey uses horse racing metaphors throughout.
   243→This is intentional: vivid domain vocabulary helps humans hold complex systems in working memory.
   244→
   245→* {jjdhr_heat} - a bounded initiative (like a race)
   246→* {jjdpr_pace} - a discrete action (the rhythm of the race)
   247→* {jjdkr_tack} - plan refinement (equipment; also "tacking" = adjusting course)
   248→* {jjdt_firemark} - heat identity, `₣`-prefixed (hot-iron brand marking ownership)
   249→* {jjdt_coronet} - pace identity, `₢`-prefixed (marking at the hoof line)
   250→* {jjdt_silks} - display name (jockey's colors)
   251→* {jjdgr_gallops} - the training grounds (where real work happens before race day)
   252→
   253→== Types
   254→
   255→[[jjdt_firemark]]
   256→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   257→{jjdt_firemark}::
   258→The identity of a
   259→{jjdhr_heat}.
   260→Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters (e.g., `₣AB`).
   261→The 2 base64 characters encode an integer 0-4095 (2^12).
   262→Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   263→Input may omit the `₣` prefix; output always includes it.
   264→
   265→[[jjdt_coronet]]
   266→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   267→{jjdt_coronet}::
   268→The identity of a
   269→{jjdpr_pace}.
   270→Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters (e.g., `₢ABCDE`).
   271→Globally unique: the first 2 base64 characters are the parent
   272→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   273→encoded identity; the remaining 3 encode the pace index (0-262143, 2^18).
   274→Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   275→Input may omit the `₢` prefix; output always includes it.
   276→+
   277→*Reassignment:* When a
   278→{jjdpr_pace}
   279→is moved between
   280→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   281→via
   282→{jjdo_draft},
   283→the old
   284→{jjdt_coronet}
   285→becomes invalid and a new one is assigned using the destination
   286→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   287→{jjdhm_seed}.
   288→The new
   289→{jjdt_coronet}
   290→embeds the destination
   291→{jjdt_firemark}.
   292→{jjdkr_tack}
   293→history is preserved, with a note recording the original
   294→{jjdt_coronet}.
   295→
   296→[[jjdt_silks]]
   297→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   298→{jjdt_silks}::
   299→A kebab-case display name.
   300→Used for human-readable identification of
   301→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   302→and
   303→{jjdpr_pace_s}.
   304→Pattern: `[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`
   305→
   306→== Records
   307→
   308→=== {jjdgr_gallops}
   309→
   310→[[jjdgr_gallops]]
   311→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   312→{jjdgr_gallops}::
   313→The root JSON structure containing all Job Jockey data.
   314→Singleton record persisted at `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.
   315→
   316→==== Members
   317→
   318→* [[jjdgm_seed]]
   319→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   320→{jjdgm_seed}
   321→is the next
   322→{jjdt_firemark}
   323→to allocate, stored as 2 base64 characters.
   324→Incremented by
   325→{jjdo_nominate}.
   326→
   327→=== {jjdhr_heat}
   328→
   329→[[jjdhr_heat]]
   330→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   331→{jjdhr_heat}::
   332→A bounded initiative with coherent goals.
   333→Contains zero or more
   334→{jjdpr_pace_s}
   335→representing discrete actions.
   336→Keyed in JSON by
   337→{jjdt_firemark}.
   338→
   339→==== Members
   340→
   341→* [[jjdhm_firemark]]
   342→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   343→{jjdhm_firemark}
   344→is the
   345→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   346→primary key.
   347→Type:
   348→{jjdt_firemark}.
   349→Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.
   350→
   351→* [[jjdhm_silks]]
   352→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   353→{jjdhm_silks}
   354→is the
   355→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   356→display name.
   357→Type:
   358→{jjdt_silks}.
   359→
   360→* [[jjdhm_created]]
   361→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   362→{jjdhm_created}
   363→records when the
   364→{jjdhr_heat}
   365→was nominated.
   366→Format: YYMMDD.
   367→
   368→* [[jjdhm_status]]
   369→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   370→{jjdhm_status}
   371→indicates the
   372→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   373→lifecycle state.
   374→Values:
   375→{jjdhe_racing},
   376→{jjdhe_stabled},
   377→or
   378→{jjdhe_retired}.
   379→
   380→* [[jjdhm_order]]
   381→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeated⟧
   382→{jjdhm_order}
   383→lists
   384→{jjdt_coronet_s}
   385→in execution sequence.
   386→May be reordered via
   387→{jjdo_rail}.
   388→
   389→* [[jjdhm_seed]]
   390→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   391→{jjdhm_seed}
   392→is the next
   393→{jjdt_coronet}
   394→index to allocate, stored as 3 base64 characters.
   395→Incremented by
   396→{jjdo_slate}.
   397→
   398→* [[jjdhm_paddock]]
   399→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   400→{jjdhm_paddock}
   401→is the path to the paddock file containing
   402→{jjdhr_heat}
   403→context and background.
   404→Format: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the base64-encoded
   405→{jjdt_firemark}
   406→(without `₣` prefix).
   407→Created by
   408→{jjdo_nominate}.
   409→
   410→==== Status Values
   411→
   412→[[jjdhe_racing]]
   413→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   414→{jjdhe_racing}::
   415→The
   416→{jjdhr_heat}
   417→is actively being worked.
   418→
   419→[[jjdhe_stabled]]
   420→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   421→{jjdhe_stabled}::
   422→The
   423→{jjdhr_heat}
   424→is paused, not actively worked.
   425→
   426→[[jjdhe_retired]]
   427→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   428→{jjdhe_retired}::
   429→The
   430→{jjdhr_heat}
   431→is complete and archived.
   432→
   433→=== {jjdpr_pace}
   434→
   435→[[jjdpr_pace]]
   436→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   437→{jjdpr_pace}::
   438→A discrete action within a
   439→{jjdhr_heat}.
   440→Contains one or more
   441→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   442→representing state snapshots and plan refinements.
   443→Current state is derived from `tacks[0].state`.
   444→Current silks is derived from `tacks[0].silks`.
   445→Keyed in JSON by
   446→{jjdt_coronet}.
   447→
   448→==== Members
   449→
   450→* [[jjdpm_coronet]]
   451→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧

   460→=== {jjdkr_tack}
   461→
   462→[[jjdkr_tack]]
   463→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   464→{jjdkr_tack}::
   465→A snapshot of
   466→{jjdpr_pace}
   467→state and plan at a point in time.
   468→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   469→form an append-only history capturing both plan refinements and state transitions.
   470→Position 0 is the most recent; older
   471→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   472→follow.
   473→New
   474→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   475→are prepended for cleaner git diffs.
   476→The current
   477→{jjdpr_pace}
   478→state is always
   479→`tacks[0].state`.

   481→==== Members
   482→
   483→* [[jjdkm_refined]]
   484→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   485→{jjdkm_refined}
   486→records when this
   487→{jjdkr_tack}
   488→was added.
   489→Format: YYMMDD-HHMM.
   490→
   491→* [[jjdkm_state]]
   492→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   493→{jjdkm_state}
   494→indicates the
   495→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   496→progress at this snapshot.
   497→Values:
   498→{jjdpe_rough},
   499→{jjdpe_bridled},
   500→{jjdpe_complete},
   501→or
   502→{jjdpe_abandoned}.
   503→
   504→* [[jjdkm_text]]
   505→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   506→{jjdkm_text}
   507→contains the plan specification.
   508→Non-empty string describing what the
   509→{jjdpr_pace}
   510→should accomplish.
   511→
   512→* [[jjdkm_silks]]
   513→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   514→{jjdkm_silks}
   515→is the
   516→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   517→display name at this point in time.
   518→Type:
   519→{jjdt_silks}.
   520→Enables rename history; current pace silks is derived from `tacks[0].silks`.
   521→
   522→* [[jjdkm_commit]]
   523→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   524→{jjdkm_commit}
   525→is the commit SHA at
   526→{jjdkr_tack}
   527→creation.
   528→Format: 7-character hex string.
   529→Captures repo state when plan was written.
   530→Value `0000000` indicates commit was unavailable at creation time.
   531→
   532→* [[jjdkm_direction]]
   533→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_conditional⟧
   534→{jjdkm_direction}
   535→contains execution guidance for autonomous operation.
   536→Required when
   537→{jjdkm_state}
   538→is
   539→{jjdpe_bridled};
   540→forbidden otherwise.
   541→Describes which agent to dispatch, parallelism strategy, or other execution parameters.

   548→The
   549→{jjdpr_pace}

   582→==== State Classification Predicates
   583→
   584→Predicates for querying pace states by semantic category.
   585→
   586→[[jjdpe_defined]] {jjdpe_defined}:: Pace contributes to heat's scope.
   587→* rough: true
   588→* bridled: true
   589→* complete: true
   590→* abandoned: false
   591→
   592→[[jjdpe_resolved]] {jjdpe_resolved}:: Pace requires no further action.
   593→* rough: false
   594→* bridled: false
   595→* complete: true
   596→* abandoned: true
   597→
   598→Future states declare their predicate values when added. Query logic uses predicates, not state names.

   600→== Serialization
   601→
   602→[[jjdz_charset]]
   603→{jjdz_charset}::
   604→The 64-character alphabet for identity encoding.
   605→Uses URL-safe base64 (RFC 4648 §5):
   606→`ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_`
   607→Position in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ..., `-`=62, `_`=63).
   608→This alphabet is safe for JSON keys, URLs, shell, git refs, and filenames.
   609→Matches Rust `base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD`.
   610→
   611→[[jjdz_encoding]]
   612→{jjdz_encoding}::
   613→The algorithm for encoding
   614→{jjdt_firemark}
   615→and
   616→{jjdt_coronet}
   617→values.
   618→+
   619→*{jjdt_firemark} (Heat identity):*
   620→+
   621→. Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters
   622→. Given integer H (0-4095): encode as `₣` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]`
   623→. To decode `₣XY`: H = pos(X) * 64 + pos(Y)
   624→+
   625→*{jjdt_coronet} (Pace identity):*
   626→+
   627→. Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters
   628→. First 2 characters encode the parent Heat (same as {jjdt_firemark} without prefix)
   629→. Last 3 characters encode the pace index P (0-262143)
   630→. Given Heat H and pace index P: encode as `₢` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]` + `charset[P/4096]` + `charset[(P/64)%64]` + `charset[P%64]`
   631→. To decode `₢XYZAB`: H from XY, P = pos(Z) * 4096 + pos(A) * 64 + pos(B)
   632→+
   633→*Input flexibility:*
   634→+
   635→CLI commands accept identities with or without prefix.
   636→Length determines type: 2 base64 chars = {jjdt_firemark}, 5 base64 chars = {jjdt_coronet}.
   637→Output always includes the prefix (`₣` or `₢`).

   843→== Commit Message Architecture
   844→
   845→All JJ commits include an action code that serves multiple purposes:
   846→- **Parsing steeplechase history**: Distinguishes between different operation types
   847→- **Semantic clarity**: Indicates the nature and context of each commit
   848→- **Consistency**: Applies uniformly across pace-level and heat-level operations
   849→
   850→== Routines
   851→
   852→Routines are internal reusable procedures invoked by operations.
   853→They encapsulate common logic for consistent behavior across the codebase.
   854→
   855→[[jjdr_load]]
   856→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine⟧
   857→=== {jjdr_load}
   858→
   859→Load and validate the
   860→{jjdgr_gallops}
   861→file.
   862→Every operation that reads
   863→{jjdgr_gallops}
   864→invokes this routine.
   865→Returns a validated
   866→{jjdgr_gallops}
   867→only after all checks pass.
   868→
   869→{jjds_arguments}
   870→
   871→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   872→* {jjda_file}
   873→
   874→{jjds_behavior}
   875→
   876→. Read file contents as raw bytes
   877→. Deserialize bytes into
   878→{jjdgr_gallops}
   879→structure
   880→. Reserialize
   881→{jjdgr_gallops}
   882→structure to JSON string
   883→. Compare reserialized bytes against original bytes
   884→. If different:
   885→{axc_fatal}
   886→with diagnostic showing first difference location
   887→. Validate semantic constraints (timestamps, kebab-case silks, invariants)
   888→. If validation fails:
   889→{axc_fatal}
   890→with validation errors
   891→. Return validated
   892→{jjdgr_gallops}
   893→
   894→{jjds_stdout} None (returns structure to caller)
   895→
   896→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero on validation failure or I/O error.
   897→
   898→*Architectural constraint:*
   899→{jjdr_load}
   900→is the ONLY way to obtain a
   901→{jjdgr_gallops}
   902→from disk.
   903→This makes validation bypass architecturally impossible.
   904→The round-trip check (steps 3-5) ensures the file is in canonical form.
   905→The semantic validation (steps 6-7) ensures business rules are satisfied.
   906→
   907→[[jjdr_save]]
   908→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine⟧
   909→=== {jjdr_save}
   910→
   911→Persist
   912→{jjdgr_gallops}
   913→to disk with validation.
   914→Every operation that writes
   915→{jjdgr_gallops}
   916→invokes this routine.
   917→Validates output by loading it back before finalizing.
   918→
   919→{jjds_arguments}
   920→
   921→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   922→*
   923→{jjdgr_gallops}
   924→(in-memory structure to persist)
   925→
   926→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   927→* {jjda_file}
   928→
   929→{jjds_behavior}
   930→
   931→. Serialize
   932→{jjdgr_gallops}
   933→to canonical JSON string
   934→. Write JSON to temporary file (atomic write preparation)
   935→. Call
   936→{jjdr_load}
   937→on temporary file to validate what was written
   938→. If
   939→{jjdr_load}
   940→fails: delete temporary file,
   941→{axc_fatal}
   942→with error
   943→. Rename temporary file to final path (atomic commit)
   944→
   945→{jjds_stdout} None
   946→
   947→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero on validation failure or I/O error.
   948→
   949→*Design rationale:*
   950→{jjdr_save}
   951→reuses
   952→{jjdr_load}
   953→for validation, ensuring a single validation code path for both reading and writing.
   954→If an operation produces invalid output, the save fails before corrupting the file.
   955→
   956→[[jjdr_persist]]
   957→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine⟧
   958→=== {jjdr_persist}
   959→
   960→Persist
   961→{jjdgr_gallops}
   962→state and commit changes to git with a JJ-aware commit message.
   963→This routine combines
   964→{jjdr_save}
   965→with git commit operations to maintain the journalling discipline.
   966→Used by most write operations to ensure every
   967→{jjdgr_gallops}
   968→mutation is tracked in git history.
   969→
   970→{jjds_arguments}
   971→
   972→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   973→* lock (lock handle) — acquired lock from `vvg_lock_acquire`
   974→
   975→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   976→*
   977→{jjdgr_gallops}
   978→(in-memory structure to persist)
   979→
   980→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   981→* {jjda_file}
   982→
   983→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   984→* identity —
   985→{jjdt_firemark}
   986→for heat-level operations or
   987→{jjdt_coronet}
   988→for pace-level operations
   989→
   990→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   991→* message — commit message subject (without JJ prefix or Co-Authored-By)
   992→
   993→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   994→* size_limit — maximum commit size in bytes for size guard check
   995→
   996→{jjds_behavior}
   997→
   998→.
   999→{jjdr_save}
  1000→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1001→to
  1002→{jjda_file}
  1003→. Stage gallops file with `git add {file}`
  1004→. If paddock file exists for this identity, stage it with `git add {paddock_file}`
  1005→. Run size guard check with provided size_limit
  1006→. Determine action code:
  1007→.. If identity is
  1008→{jjdt_firemark}:
  1009→extract from message prefix (`N:`, `S:`, `r:`, `T:`, `D:`, `R:`)
  1010→.. If identity is
  1011→{jjdt_coronet}:
  1012→extract from message prefix (`n:`, `A:`, `W:`, `F:`, `d:`)
  1013→. Format full commit message: `jjb:HALLMARK:IDENTITY:ACTION: message\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>`
  1014→. Run `git commit -m "..."`
  1015→. Return commit hash
  1016→
  1017→{jjds_stdout} Commit hash (returned to caller)
  1018→
  1019→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero on error.
  1020→
  1021→*Commands that use
  1022→{jjdr_persist}:*
  1023→
  1024→*
  1025→{jjdo_nominate}
  1026→— creates new heat with action `N`
  1027→*
  1028→{jjdo_slate}
  1029→— adds new pace with action `S`
  1030→*
  1031→{jjdo_rail}
  1032→— reorders paces with action `r`
  1033→*
  1034→{jjdo_tally}
  1035→— adds new tack with action `T`
  1036→*
  1037→{jjdo_draft}
  1038→— moves pace between heats with action `D`
  1039→*
  1040→{jjdo_furlough}
  1041→— changes heat status (action varies)
  1042→
  1043→*Commands that do NOT use
  1044→{jjdr_persist}:*
  1045→
  1046→*
  1047→{jjdo_retire}
  1048→— uses custom commit logic for trophy archival (stages trophy file, gallops, and paddock deletion; uses 200KB limit)
  1049→*
  1050→{jjdo_chalk}
  1051→— creates empty commits only, no gallops changes
  1052→*
  1053→{jjdo_notch}
  1054→— commits work artifacts, not gallops state
  1055→
  1056→*Design rationale:*
  1057→{jjdr_persist}
  1058→ensures consistent commit message formatting and git discipline across all
  1059→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1060→mutations.
  1061→By centralizing the commit logic, we guarantee that every state change is properly journalled with parseable commit messages for
  1062→{jjdo_rein}
  1063→to reconstruct steeplechase history.
  1064→
  1065→[[jjdr_wrap]]
  1066→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine⟧
  1067→=== {jjdr_wrap}
  1068→
  1069→Mark a pace complete and commit all changes in one self-contained operation.
  1070→This routine combines staging, committing, state transition, and chalk marker creation.
  1071→Used by `/jjc-pace-wrap` to provide a single-command wrap experience.
  1072→
  1073→{jjds_arguments}
  1074→
  1075→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1076→* {jjdt_coronet}
  1077→(positional) — pace to wrap
  1078→
  1079→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1080→* `--size-limit` — maximum commit size in bytes (default: 50KB)
  1081→
  1082→{jjds_behavior}
  1083→
  1084→. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
  1085→. Stage all changes (`git add -A`)
  1086→. Size guard check:
  1087→.. Get staged content size
  1088→.. If over limit: release lock, exit 2
  1089→. Generate commit message via Claude from diff
  1090→. Commit staged changes
  1091→. Transition pace state to complete via
  1092→{jjdo_tally}
  1093→. Create W chalk marker with outcome summary
  1094→. Release lock
  1095→. Return commit hash
  1096→
  1097→{jjds_exit_enumerated}
  1098→
  1099→* 0 — success
  1100→* 1 — general error (parse failure, gallops error, git error)
  1101→* 2 — size guard exceeded (use --size-limit to override)
  1102→
  1103→*Design rationale:*
  1104→{jjdr_wrap}
  1105→consolidates the multi-step wrap workflow into a single vvx command.
  1106→This eliminates the need for slash commands to orchestrate separate notch, tally, and chalk operations.
  1107→The distinct exit code 2 for size guard allows callers to advise users about the --size-limit flag.
  1108→
  1109→== Operations

  1116→and
  1117→{jjdr_save}.
  1118→Bash handles locking via `git update-ref` and invokes {jjdx_cli} with
  1119→{jjda_file}.

  1367→[[jjdo_rail]]
  1368→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1369→==== {jjdo_rail}
  1370→
  1371→Reorder
  1372→{jjdpr_pace_s}
  1373→within a
  1374→{jjdhr_heat}
  1375→by updating
  1376→{jjdhm_order}.
  1377→Supports two modes: *order mode* replaces the entire sequence; *move mode* relocates a single
  1378→{jjdpr_pace}.
  1379→
  1380→*Order mode* (full replacement):
  1381→----
  1382→vvx jjx_rail <FIREMARK> <CORONET>...
  1383→----
  1384→
  1385→*Move mode* (single relocation):
  1386→----
  1387→vvx jjx_rail <FIREMARK> --move <CORONET> [--before <TARGET> | --after <TARGET> | --first | --last]
  1388→----
  1389→
  1390→{jjds_arguments}
  1391→
  1392→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1393→* {jjda_file}
  1394→
  1395→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1396→* {jjdt_firemark}
  1397→(positional) — target
  1398→{jjdhr_heat}
  1399→
  1400→*Order mode arguments:*
  1401→
  1402→// ⟦axd_required⟧

  1404→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1405→in desired sequence (space-separated positional arguments)
  1406→
  1407→*Move mode arguments:*
  1408→
  1409→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1410→* {jjda_move}
  1411→— {jjdt_coronet}
  1412→to relocate
  1413→
  1414→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1415→* Exactly one positioning flag:
  1416→{jjda_before},
  1417→{jjda_after},
  1418→{jjda_first},
  1419→or
  1420→{jjda_last}
  1421→
  1422→*Mode detection:* If
  1423→{jjda_move}
  1424→is present, move mode; otherwise order mode.
  1425→
  1426→{jjds_stdout} Updated
  1427→{jjdhm_order}
  1428→array, one
  1429→{jjdt_coronet}
  1430→per line, in new sequence.
  1431→
  1432→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1433→
  1434→{jjds_behavior}
  1435→
  1436→*Common steps:*
  1437→
  1438→.
  1439→{jjdr_load}
  1440→{jjda_file};
  1441→on failure, exit immediately with
  1442→{jjdr_load}
  1443→error status
  1444→. Verify
  1445→{jjdhr_heat}
  1446→exists with given
  1447→{jjdt_firemark}
  1448→
  1449→*Order mode:*
  1450→
  1451→. Parse positional arguments into array of
  1452→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1453→. Validate order array:
  1454→.. Count must match current
  1455→{jjdhm_order}
  1456→length
  1457→.. No duplicate
  1458→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1459→.. All
  1460→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1461→must exist in
  1462→{jjdhr_heat_p}
  1463→`paces` object
  1464→.. All
  1465→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1466→must embed correct parent
  1467→{jjdt_firemark}
  1468→. Replace
  1469→{jjdhm_order}
  1470→with new order array
  1471→.
  1472→{jjdr_save}
  1473→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1474→to
  1475→{jjda_file}
  1476→. Output new
  1477→{jjdhm_order},
  1478→one
  1479→{jjdt_coronet}
  1480→per line
  1481→
  1482→*Move mode:*
  1483→
  1484→. Validate
  1485→{jjda_move}
  1486→{jjdt_coronet}
  1487→exists in
  1488→{jjdhr_heat}

  1491→{jjda_before}

  1493→{jjda_after}:
  1494→validate target
  1495→{jjdt_coronet}
  1496→exists and differs from
  1497→{jjda_move}
  1498→{jjdt_coronet}
  1499→. Remove
  1500→{jjda_move}
  1501→{jjdt_coronet}
  1502→from current position in
  1503→{jjdhm_order}
  1504→. Insert at new position:
  1505→.. If
  1506→{jjda_first}:
  1507→insert before first actionable pace (or append if none)
  1508→.. If
  1509→{jjda_last}:
  1510→append to end
  1511→.. If
  1512→{jjda_before}:
  1513→insert immediately before target
  1514→{jjdt_coronet}
  1515→.. If
  1516→{jjda_after}:
  1517→insert immediately after target
  1518→{jjdt_coronet}
  1519→.
  1520→{jjdr_save}
  1521→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1522→to
  1523→{jjda_file}
  1524→. Output new
  1525→{jjdhm_order},
  1526→one
  1527→{jjdt_coronet}
  1528→per line

  2113→[[jjdo_validate]]

  2118→{jjdgr_gallops}
  2119→JSON conforms to schema.

  2125→
  2126→{jjds_stdout} None
  2127→
  2128→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  2129→
  2130→*Validation Rules:*
  2131→
  2132→. Root must be JSON object with
  2133→{jjdgm_seed}
  2134→(2 URL-safe base64 characters matching `[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2}`)
  2135→. Root must have `heats` object
  2136→. Each
  2137→{jjdhr_heat}
  2138→key must match `₣[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2}`
  2139→. Each
  2140→{jjdhr_heat}
  2141→must have:
  2142→{jjdhm_silks}
  2143→(non-empty kebab-case),
  2144→{jjdhm_created}
  2145→(YYMMDD),
  2146→{jjdhm_status}
  2147→({jjdhe_racing},
  2148→{jjdhe_stabled},
  2149→or
  2150→{jjdhe_retired}),
  2151→{jjdhm_order}
  2152→(array),
  2153→{jjdhm_seed}
  2154→(3 URL-safe base64 characters matching `[A-Za-z0-9_-]{3}`),
  2155→`paces` (object)
  2156→. {jjdhm_order}
  2157→array and `paces` object must have identical key sets
  2158→. Each
  2159→{jjdpr_pace}
  2160→key must match `₢[A-Za-z0-9_-]{5}` and embed parent
  2161→{jjdhr_heat}
  2162→identity (first 2 base64 chars after prefix)
  2163→. Each
  2164→{jjdpr_pace}
  2165→must have:
  2166→`tacks` (non-empty array)
  2167→. Each
  2168→{jjdkr_tack}
  2169→must have:
  2170→{jjdkm_refined}
  2171→(YYMMDD-HHMM),
  2172→{jjdkm_state}
  2173→({jjdpe_rough},
  2174→{jjdpe_bridled},
  2175→{jjdpe_complete},
  2176→or
  2177→{jjdpe_abandoned}),
  2178→{jjdkm_text}
  2179→(non-empty string),
  2180→{jjdkm_silks}
  2181→(non-empty kebab-case),
  2182→{jjdkm_commit}
  2183→(valid {jjdkm_commit} format)
  2184→. If
  2185→{jjdkm_state}
  2186→is
  2187→{jjdpe_bridled},
  2188→{jjdkm_direction}
  2189→must be present (non-empty string); otherwise
  2190→{jjdkm_direction}
  2191→must be absent

  2241→{jjdpr_pace}

  2243→{jjdhm_order}
  2244→whose state is
  2245→{jjdpe_rough}

  2259→(positional) — target

  2262→{jjds_stdout} JSON object with structure:
  2263→[source,json]
  2264→----

  2288→
  2289→{jjds_behavior}
  2290→
  2291→. Read
  2292→{jjdgr_gallops}
  2293→from file
  2294→. Verify
  2295→{jjdhr_heat}
  2296→exists with given
  2297→{jjdt_firemark}
  2298→. Error if
  2299→{jjdhm_status}
  2300→is
  2301→{jjdhe_stabled}
  2302→("Cannot saddle stabled heat {firemark}")
  2303→. Output `heat_silks` from
  2304→{jjdhr_heat}

  2308→. Walk
  2309→{jjdhm_order}
  2310→array in sequence
  2311→. For each
  2312→{jjdt_coronet},

  2315→{jjdpe_rough}

  2321→.. Output `spec` from `tacks[0].text`

  2327→. Get last 10 steeplechase entries for the heat via

  2329→logic, output as `recent_work` array

  2331→[[jjdo_scout]]
  2332→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  2333→==== {jjdo_scout}
  2334→
  2335→Search across
  2336→{jjdhr_heat_s}
  2337→and
  2338→{jjdpr_pace_s}
  2339→using regex patterns.
  2340→
  2341→{jjds_arguments}
  2342→
  2343→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  2344→* {jjda_file}
  2345→
  2346→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2347→* pattern (positional) — regex pattern to search for (case insensitive)
  2348→
  2349→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  2350→* `--actionable` — limit results to
  2351→{jjdpe_rough}
  2352→or
  2353→{jjdpe_bridled}
  2354→paces only
  2355→
  2356→{jjds_stdout} Grouped text output:
  2357→----
  2358→₣XX heat-silks
  2359→  ₢XXXXX [state] pace-silks
  2360→    field: ...match excerpt...
  2361→₣YY another-heat
  2362→  ₢YYYYY [state] another-pace
  2363→    field: ...match excerpt...
  2364→----
  2365→
  2366→Where `field` is one of: `silks`, `spec`, `direction`, or `paddock`.
  2367→Match excerpt shows approximately 60 characters of context around the first match.
  2368→
  2369→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  2370→
  2371→{jjds_behavior}
  2372→
  2373→. Read
  2374→{jjdgr_gallops}
  2375→from file
  2376→. Build case-insensitive regex from pattern argument
  2377→. For each
  2378→{jjdhr_heat}
  2379→(all statuses: racing, stabled, retired):
  2380→.. For each
  2381→{jjdpr_pace}
  2382→in
  2383→{jjdhm_order}:
  2384→... If `--actionable` specified, skip paces not in
  2385→{jjdpe_rough}
  2386→or
  2387→{jjdpe_bridled}
  2388→state
  2389→... Search in order (first match wins):
  2390→.... {jjdkm_silks}
  2391→(from `tacks[0].silks`)
  2392→.... {jjdkm_text}
  2393→(spec from `tacks[0].text`)
  2394→.... {jjdkm_direction}
  2395→(from `tacks[0].direction` if present)
  2396→.... {jjdhm_paddock}
  2397→file content
  2398→... If match found:
  2399→.... If this is the first match in this
  2400→{jjdhr_heat},
  2401→print heat header
  2402→.... Print pace line with
  2403→{jjdt_coronet},
  2404→state, and
  2405→{jjdkm_silks}
  2406→.... Print match line with field name and context excerpt (60 chars around match)
  2407→
  2408→*Design rationale:*
  2409→{jjdo_scout}
  2410→does NOT search steeplechase/chalk descriptions.
  2411→Steeplechase entries are for session history reconstruction; searching them would conflate "what we planned" with "what we discussed."
  2412→The scout searches plan artifacts only:
  2413→{jjdkm_silks},
  2414→{jjdkm_text},
  2415→{jjdkm_direction},
  2416→and
  2417→{jjdhm_paddock}
  2418→content.
  2419→
  2420→[[jjdo_parade]]
  2421→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  2422→==== {jjdo_parade}
  2423→
  2424→Display
  2425→{jjdhr_heat}
  2426→or
  2427→{jjdpr_pace}
  2428→information in human-readable text format.
  2429→Output varies by target type.
  2430→
  2431→{jjds_arguments}
  2432→
  2433→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  2434→* {jjda_file}
  2435→
  2436→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2437→* TARGET (positional) —
  2438→{jjdt_firemark}
  2439→(heat view) or
  2440→{jjdt_coronet}
  2441→(pace view).
  2442→Target type detected by length: 2 base64 chars = heat, 5 base64 chars = pace.
  2443→
  2444→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  2445→* `--full` — Show paddock and full pace specs (heat mode only)
  2446→
  2447→// ⟦axd_optional⟧

  2449→{jjdpe_complete}
  2450→and

  2453→{jjds_stdout} Human-readable text (not JSON).

  2455→*Example output — heat list (default):*
  2456→----

  2554→=== Steeplechase Operations
  2555→
  2556→The steeplechase is Job Jockey's session history, stored in git commits rather than the
  2557→{jjdgr_gallops}
  2558→JSON.
  2559→Commit messages follow patterns that
  2560→{jjdo_rein}
  2561→can parse to reconstruct session history.
  2562→
  2563→==== Commit Message Patterns
  2564→
  2565→All JJ commits use the format: `jjb:HALLMARK:IDENTITY:ACTION: message`
  2566→
  2567→* `jjb` — JJ brand prefix (literal)
  2568→* `HALLMARK` — Version identifier. Format: NNNN (installed) or NNNN-xxxxxxx (dev). Source: `.vvk/vvbf_brand.json` (installed) or max hallmark from `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/vovr_registry.json` + git HEAD (Kit Forge)
  2569→* `IDENTITY` — ₢CORONET for pace-level, ₣FIREMARK for heat-level
  2570→* `ACTION` — single-letter code (required for all commits)
  2571→
  2572→*Standard notch* (created by {jjdo_notch}):
  2573→----
  2574→jjb:HALLMARK:₢XXXXX:n: commit message
  2575→
  2576→Co-Authored-By: ...
  2577→----
  2578→
  2579→Standard notch commits use `n` to denote routine progress tracking.
  2580→
  2581→*Pace-level chalk* (created by {jjdo_chalk}):
  2582→----
  2583→jjb:HALLMARK:₢XXXXX:A: description
  2584→
  2585→Co-Authored-By: ...
  2586→----
  2587→
  2588→Pace-level action codes:
  2589→
  2590→* `n` — Notch: standard commit
  2591→* `A` — APPROACH: proposed approach before work begins
  2592→* `W` — WRAP: pace completion summary
  2593→* `F` — FLY: autonomous execution began (bridled pace)
  2594→* `d` — discussion: significant decision (lowercase, can also be heat-level)
  2595→
  2596→*Heat-level operations*:
  2597→----
  2598→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:N: heat-silks
  2599→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:S: pace-silks
  2600→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:r: reordered
  2601→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:T: pace-silks
  2602→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:D: old-coronet → new-coronet
  2603→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:R: heat-silks
  2604→----
  2605→
  2606→Heat-level action codes:
  2607→
  2608→* `N` — Nominate: create new heat
  2609→* `S` — Slate: add new pace
  2610→* `r` — rail: reorder paces (lowercase)
  2611→* `T` — Tally: add tack to pace
  2612→* `D` — Draft: move pace between heats (uppercase, rare)
  2613→* `R` — Retire: archive heat (uppercase)
  2614→
  2615→[[jjdo_rein]]
  2616→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  2617→==== {jjdo_rein}
  2618→
  2619→Parse git history for steeplechase entries belonging to a
  2620→{jjdhr_heat}.
  2621→
  2622→{jjds_arguments}
  2623→
  2624→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2625→* {jjdt_firemark}
  2626→(positional) — target
  2627→{jjdhr_heat}
  2628→
  2629→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  2630→* `--limit` — maximum entries to return (default: 50)
  2631→
  2632→{jjds_stdout} JSON array of steeplechase entries:
  2633→[source,json]
  2634→----
  2635→[
  2636→  {
  2637→    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  2638→    "commit": "abc123ef",
  2639→    "hallmark": "1009",
  2640→    "coronet": "₢XXXXX",
  2641→    "action": "n",
  2642→    "subject": "commit message"
  2643→  },
  2644→  {
  2645→    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  2646→    "commit": "def456ab",
  2647→    "hallmark": "1009-abc1234",
  2648→    "coronet": "₢XXXXX",
  2649→    "action": "A",
  2650→    "subject": "description"
  2651→  },
  2652→  {
  2653→    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  2654→    "hallmark": "1009",
  2655→    "action": "N",
  2656→    "subject": "heat-silks"
  2657→  }
  2658→]
  2659→----
  2660→Field `hallmark` is always present.
  2661→Field `coronet` is present for pace-level entries, absent for heat-level operations.
  2662→Field `action` is present for all commits, including standard notch commits (`n`).
  2663→Entries ordered newest first.
  2664→
  2665→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  2666→
  2667→{jjds_behavior}
  2668→
  2669→. Run `git log --all --extended-regexp --grep="^jjb:[^:]+:(₣XX|₢XX)" --format="%ai%x09%s"`
  2670→  (skips hallmark field; filtering is by identity)
  2671→. Parse each line: extract timestamp, hallmark, identity, action, and message
  2672→. For pace-level (₢CORONET): verify coronet belongs to target heat
  2673→. For heat-level (₣FIREMARK): verify firemark matches target heat
  2674→. Build JSON array, limit to requested count
  2675→. Output JSON
  2676→
  2677→[[jjdo_notch]]
  2678→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  2679→==== {jjdo_notch}
  2680→
  2681→Create a JJ-aware git commit with heat/pace context prefix.
  2682→
  2683→{jjds_arguments}
  2684→
  2685→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2686→* identity (positional) — {jjdt_coronet} for pace-affiliated commit, or {jjdt_firemark} for heat-only commit
  2687→
  2688→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2689→* files (positional, repeated) — paths to commit (at least one required)
  2690→
  2691→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  2692→* `--size-limit` — size limit in bytes for the commit guard (default: 50KB)
  2693→
  2694→{jjds_stdout} Commit hash
  2695→
  2696→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  2697→
  2698→{jjds_behavior}
  2699→
  2700→. Parse identity:
  2701→.. If 5 base64 chars (Coronet): pace-affiliated commit
  2702→.. If 2 base64 chars (Firemark): heat-only commit (no pace affiliation)
  2703→. Validate file list:
  2704→.. If empty: exit 1 with "error: no files specified"
  2705→.. If any file does not exist: exit 1 with "error: file not found: {path}"
  2706→. Check for uncommitted changes outside file list:
  2707→.. Run `git status --porcelain` to get all modified files
  2708→.. For each modified file NOT in the specified list, warn to stderr:
  2709→+
  2710→----
  2711→warning: uncommitted changes outside file list:
  2712→  modified: path/to/file.rs
  2713→----
  2714→. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
  2715→. Stage specified files only (`git add <file1> <file2> ...`)
  2716→. Run size guard check (uses `--size-limit` if provided, otherwise 50KB default)
  2717→. Run `claude --print "Generate commit message for this diff: $(git diff --cached)"`
  2718→. Format full message based on identity type:
  2719→.. Coronet: `jjb:HALLMARK:₢XXXXX:n: message\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
  2720→.. Firemark: `jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:n: message\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
  2721→. Run `git commit -m "..."`
  2722→. Release lock
  2723→. Output commit hash
  2724→
  2725→[[jjdo_chalk]]
  2726→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  2727→==== {jjdo_chalk}
  2728→
  2729→Create an empty commit marking a steeplechase event.
  2730→
  2731→{jjds_arguments}
  2732→
  2733→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2734→* identity (positional) — {jjdt_coronet} for pace-level, {jjdt_firemark} for heat-level discussion
  2735→
  2736→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2737→* `--marker` — marker type: `A`, `W`, `F`, or `d` (single letter codes)
  2738→
  2739→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2740→* `--description` — marker description text
  2741→
  2742→{jjds_stdout} Commit hash
  2743→
  2744→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  2745→
  2746→{jjds_behavior}
  2747→
  2748→. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
  2749→. Detect identity type by length (5 chars = coronet, 2 chars = firemark)
  2750→. Validate: `A`/`W`/`F` require coronet; `d` accepts either
  2751→. Format message:
  2752→.. If coronet: `jjb:HALLMARK:₢XXXXX:X: description\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
  2753→.. If firemark (discussion only): `jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:d: description\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
  2754→. Run `git commit --allow-empty -m "..."`
  2755→. Release lock
  2756→. Output commit hash

[[jjdr_wrap]]
// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine⟧
=== {jjdr_wrap}

Mark a pace complete and commit all changes in one self-contained operation.
This routine combines staging, committing, state transition, and chalk marker creation.
Used by `/jjc-pace-wrap` to provide a single-command wrap experience.

{jjds_arguments}

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* {jjdt_coronet}
(positional) — pace to wrap

// ⟦axd_optional⟧
* `--size-limit` — maximum commit size in bytes (default: 50KB)

{jjds_behavior}

. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
. Stage all changes (`git add -A`)
. Size guard check:
.. Get staged content size
.. If over limit: release lock, exit 2
. Generate commit message via Claude from diff
. Commit staged changes
. Transition pace state to complete via
{jjdo_tally}
. Create W chalk marker with outcome summary
. Release lock
. Return commit hash

{jjds_exit_enumerated}

* 0 — success
* 1 — general error (parse failure, gallops error, git error)
* 2 — size guard exceeded (use --size-limit to override)

*Design rationale:*
{jjdr_wrap}
consolidates the multi-step wrap workflow into a single vvx command.
This eliminates the need for slash commands to orchestrate separate notch, tally, and chalk operations.
The distinct exit code 2 for size guard allows callers to advise users about the --size-limit flag.
